session_name_by_week.12: Python Foundations II and Calling an LLM

narrative_seed_by_week.12: Python Foundations II / Functions — named, reusable blocks of logic; parameters and return values / One function = one job — the testable unit principle / Lists and dictionaries — storing and retrieving multiple values / Reading from and writing to a file — open, read lines, write, close / try/except — handling errors gracefully without crashing /  / Calling an LLM from Python / What an API is — a door into another system; request and response / The Anthropic API — model, messages array, system prompt, user prompt, authentication / Making the first API call — install the library, write the call, run it in Colab / Parsing the JSON response — extracting the text you need / Handling API errors — what to do when the call fails / Every API call is a design decision — latency, cost, and reliability considered upfront

lab_activity_seed_by_week.12: Lab Part 1 — Functions and files: read a CSV of 10 student marks → compute average, highest, lowest → write summary file. Spec-first. Test with 2 edge cases. /  / Lab Part 2 — First API call: connect your domain system to the Anthropic API — user query → Claude → structured JSON output. Test with 5 domain-relevant inputs. Write a cost and latency note. / Journal Entry #7
